description: Update all UI components (HUD, CombatMenu, EnemyDisplay) to use generated sprites instead of ShapeRenderer rectangles and plain text. Implement animated HP/MP bars with lerp, status effect sprite icons, enemy sprite with SpriteAnimator, telegraph pulsing icon, damage flash/white flash on enemy, menu frame border with element icons, cooldown indicators, and the full battle scene layout at 320x240.
steps: Update src/view/ui/EnemyDisplay.java: Replace placeholder texture drawing with actual enemy sprite from AssetLoader.getEntityFrames(). Add a SpriteAnimator field that manages the enemy's animation state. Display enemy sprite centered horizontally, scaled 2-3x from base size. Show textured HP bar frame from AssetLoader.getHpBarFrame() above the enemy. Show status effect icons below HP bar using AssetLoader.getStatusIcon(). When telegraph is active, render a pulsing exclamation mark icon over the enemy. When triggerDamageFlash() is called, transition SpriteAnimator to HURT state. Add method setEnemyName(String) to support switching enemies between waves. Add method triggerDeathAnimation() for death sequence. Add method triggerAttackAnimation() for when enemy attacks.

Update src/view/ui/HUD.java: Replace ShapeRenderer HP/MP bars with textured bar frames from AssetLoader. Implement animated fill using lerp (smoothly animate to new HP/MP values over ~0.3s using displayedHp/displayedMp float fields). Render status effect icons as actual sprites from getStatusIcon() in a row below HP/MP. Add player mini-sprite in bottom-left corner from AssetLoader.getEntityFrames('player', IDLE). Add wave counter with decorative frame from getWaveBanner(). Render background tiles using getBackgroundTile() tiled across the 320x240 screen. Add enemy name plate text above the enemy sprite area.

Update src/view/ui/CombatMenu.java: Render pixel-art menu frame border using AssetLoader.getMenuFrame() around the action list. Add element color coding on skill names using CombatMenu.getElementColor(). Add a blinking cursor/arrow sprite for the selected item. Show element icon from AssetLoader.getElementIcon() next to each skill in sub-menu. Show mana cost visually. Add cooldown overlay (grayed-out with turn number) for skills on cooldown.

Update src/view/screens/GameScreen.java: Integrate VisualEffectsManager - create instance, update each frame, render after HUD. On GameEvent callbacks (onEvent), trigger appropriate visual effects: DAMAGE_DEALT -> spawnSlashEffect + spawnDamageNumber at enemy position, CRITICAL_HIT -> larger slash + screen shake + 'CRITICAL!' text popup, SPELL_CAST -> element-appropriate particles (fire/holy/dark/poison), ENEMY_ATTACK -> screen edge red flash + HP bar shake, ENEMY_DEFEATED -> enemy death animation + fade. Pass AssetLoader to VisualEffectsManager for particle textures. Add background tile rendering before other UI. Add torchlight flicker effect (subtle periodic brightness variation on background alpha).
acceptance_criteria: Enemy sprite renders from SpriteGenerator art (not colored rectangles) with idle animation bobbing
Enemy HP bar uses textured frame sprite, not ShapeRenderer
HP/MP bars smoothly animate (lerp) when values change
Status effect icons render as pixel art sprites, not text abbreviations
Combat menu has decorative pixel-art frame border
Skills show element icons and color coding
Damage numbers float up and fade on hit events
Particle effects spawn for fire/holy/poison/dark spells
Screen flashes red on player damage
Telegraph shows pulsing icon over enemy
Death animation plays on enemy defeat
Background tiles render behind the battle scene
verification: Run ./gradlew classes and confirm BUILD SUCCESSFUL with no errors
findings: All four files updated successfully. EnemyDisplay now uses SpriteAnimator for per-entity animation states with HURT/ATTACKING/DYING transitions, textured HP bar from getHpBarFrame(), status icons from getStatusIcon(), and pulsing telegraph. HUD uses lerp-animated HP/MP bars with textured frames, player mini-sprite, wave banner, background tiles, and sprite status icons. CombatMenu renders pixel-art menu frame, element icons next to skills, element color coding, blinking cursor, and cooldown overlay. GameScreen integrates VisualEffectsManager: update each frame, render inside SpriteBatch, trigger effects from onEvent for DAMAGE_DEALT, CRITICAL_HIT, SPELL_CAST, ENEMY_ATTACK, ENEMY_DEFEATED, WAVE_COMPLETE, HEAL events. Torchlight flicker and screen flash overlay also added. BUILD SUCCESSFUL verified.
